pageHeader.title: HTTP Data Sources
pageHeader.description: Manage HTTP data sources: add, edit and filter sources

info.title: What is an HTTP Data Source?
info.description: An HTTP Data Source enables bidirectional communication between your IoT devices and MapexOS using HTTP/HTTPS protocols. It provides a flexible way to receive telemetry data from devices or send commands to them.

pull.title: Pull Mode
pull.text: MapexOS periodically fetches data from your devices by making HTTP requests to their endpoints

push.title: Push Mode
push.text: Your devices send data directly to MapexOS endpoints whenever events occur

authentication.title: Authentication
authentication.text: Supports multiple auth methods: API Key, JWT, OAuth2, IP Whitelist, or None

assetBinding.title: Asset Binding
assetBinding.text: Link data to specific assets using Fixed Asset ID or Dynamic UUID field mapping

empty.title: No data sources found
empty.description: It seems there are no data sources matching your current search criteria or you don't have any data sources yet.

deleteDialog.title: Confirm Deletion
deleteDialog.message: Are you sure you want to delete the data source "{name}"?

errors.apiNotInitialized: HTTP Gateway API is not available
errors.noUuidPaths: No UUID paths configured to test
errors.invalidPayloadOrPath: Invalid JSON payload or path format

drawer.title: Data Source Details
drawer.error: Failed to load data source details

page.title: Add HTTP Data Source
page.description: Configure a new HTTP data source for your IoT platform
page.descriptionEdit: Update the configuration of your HTTP data source

stepper.title: HTTP Data Source Setup
stepper.subtitle: Follow the steps to configure your data source

basicInfo.title: Basic Information
basicInfo.description: Name, description and status of the data source

workingHours.title: Working Hours & Rate Limit
workingHours.description: Configure working hours and rate limit

review.title: Review
review.description: Review and confirm data source settings

progress.completeAllSteps: Complete all steps to create a new data source

protocolConfig.gatewayBannerBody: This data source uses HTTP/HTTPS protocol exclusively. Only Push mode is available where devices send data to the MapexOS endpoint.
protocolConfig.pushModeHint: Push mode: devices send data to this endpoint via HTTP POST requests.
protocolConfig.protocolFixedHint: HTTP/HTTPS protocol is fixed for this gateway type

notifications.createFailed: Failed to create HTTP Data Source
notifications.updateFailed: Failed to update HTTP Data Source
notifications.loadFailed: Failed to load HTTP Data Source data

basicInfo.name: Name
basicInfo.debugModeTooltip: Enable debug mode to log incoming requests for troubleshooting

apiKey.banner: Clients must send a valid API Key in the request header.
apiKey.headerNameHint: HTTP header name that will contain the API Key
apiKey.valueHint: Secret key that clients must provide

jwt.headerNameHint: HTTP header name that will contain the JWT token (default: Authorization)
jwt.secretKeyHint: Shared secret key for HS256 JWT validation

ipWhitelist.banner: Only requests from allowed IP addresses/ranges will be accepted.
ipWhitelist.cidrMaskHint: Subnet mask (0-32 for IPv4, 0-128 for IPv6)
ipWhitelist.emptyMessage: No IP addresses added yet. Add at least one IP address to the whitelist.
ipWhitelist.duplicateError: This IP/CIDR is already in the whitelist
ipWhitelist.invalidIpv4Cidr: IPv4 CIDR mask must be between 0 and 32
ipWhitelist.invalidIpv6Cidr: IPv6 CIDR mask must be between 0 and 128
ipWhitelist.invalidIpFormat: Invalid IP address format. Please enter a valid IPv4 or IPv6 address.

oauth2.banner: OAuth 2.0 / OpenID Connect authentication with JWKS endpoint.
oauth2.jwksUrlHint: OAuth2 JWKS endpoint URL for token validation

none.banner: This data source will accept requests from ANY client without validation. Use only for testing or in trusted networks. This is NOT recommended for production environments.

fixedAsset.banner: All data sent to this endpoint will be linked to the selected asset. Use this when you have a dedicated endpoint for a single device.

uuidField.banner: The device UUID will be extracted from the payload to identify which asset the data belongs to. Define one or more JSON paths — type a template name to auto-fill the path, or enter it manually.
uuidField.uuidJsonPathHint: Path to device UUID in payload (e.g., device.uuid or header.deviceId)
uuidField.invalidPathFormat: Invalid path format (use only letters, numbers, dots, underscores)
uuidField.noTemplatePath: This template has no UUID path configured

review.subtitle: Review your configuration before saving. You can edit any section by clicking the edit button.
review.successMessage: Ready to save! Click "Create Data Source" or "Update Data Source" to confirm your changes.
review.name: Name
review.protocolBanner: HTTP Data Sources always use HTTP protocol in Push mode where devices send data to the MapexOS endpoint.
review.noAuthWarning: No Authentication - This endpoint accepts requests without validation
